_doc: FLUX module registry — single source of truth for the modular architecture. Read by main.js (config validation, binary fetcher) and renderer.js (Settings > Modules page, tab visibility). Each module declares its tabs (so the UI can hide them when disabled), its required vendor binaries (so the first-launch fetcher downloads only what's needed), and a stable id (used as the config key for enabled/disabled state). Phase 1: code still lives in main.js + renderer.js — modules/<id>/ folders are placeholders for the Phase 2 physical refactor.
_doc_tabBinaries: Per-TAB binary requirements (finer than per-module). Drives the lazy download: a nav item shows a download arrow only when ITS tab needs binaries that aren't installed. Tabs NOT listed here need nothing to be opened (e.g. radio/rss/spotify just browse/play/parse in the renderer — recording/downloading prompts at action time). A module's full binary set still lives in modules[].binaries (used by Settings > Modules + the Identify trigger).

ffmpeg.purpose: Video/audio encode + mux + filter (trim, convert, normalize)

ffprobe.purpose: Media inspection (duration, codecs, streams) — required by yt-dlp postprocessing

fpcalc.purpose: Chromaprint audio fingerprinting for AcoustID song ID

modules.name: Core
modules.description: Home, Settings, Queue, History, Profiles, Scheduler. Always installed — provides the shared lifecycle, persistence, and proxy/notification infrastructure used by every other module.

modules.name: Media
modules.description: Download videos, audio, podcasts, livestreams, radio, RSS feeds, Spotify (via yt-dlp). Handles format presets (MP4/MKV/MP3/FLAC/M4A/Opus, 720p/1080p), batch queue, retry/resume.

modules.name: Torrent
modules.description: Search YTS, Nyaa, TPB and custom sources. Save .torrent / .magnet files, copy magnet links, or send-to-client (qBittorrent / Transmission WebUI).

modules.name: IRC / XDCC
modules.description: IRC client with SASL auth, channel browsing, private messages, and DCC/XDCC file transfers. Supports TLS + SOCKS5 proxy (Tor) for privacy.

modules.name: NZB / Usenet
modules.description: Forward .nzb files to your SABnzbd or NZBGet server. Pick a local .nzb (or drop one) and FLUX hands it off to the configured Usenet client over its HTTP API — no binaries needed.

modules.name: Tag editor
modules.description: Edit ID3v2 / Vorbis / MP4 tags on audio files. Multi-file batch edit, album art import/export, MusicBrainz lookups for auto-tagging.

modules.name: Music recognition
modules.description: Recognise the currently playing song via Shazam (no key) or AcoustID (user-provided key). Microphone capture or file-based identification.

modules.name: Convert Media
modules.description: Local media manipulation: trim, convert, extract audio, extract subtitles, split tracks, normalize loudness, frame export, concat, GIF encode. No internet required.

modules.name: Images
modules.description: Batch image operations: rename, convert (JPG/PNG/WebP/AVIF/HEIC), resize, watermark, EXIF strip, compress to size, deduplicate, FX presets. Includes single-image editor (crop, annotate).

modules.name: File & Sync
modules.description: Copy and sync folders to a device (USB, drive) with incremental / two-way / mirror modes and a FreeFileSync-style preview. Optional audio→MP3 transcode + M3U playlist for the car. Other modules surface one-click 'Sync to…' shortcuts here when enabled.

modules.name: Video Editor
modules.description: Batch operations on a folder of videos — the third editor alongside Tag Editor (audio) and Image Editor (images). Convert format, extract audio, batch rename. Needs ffmpeg + ffprobe for the conversions.
